Catholic Memorial Home

CCN: 225448 · FALL RIVER, MA 02720 · Bristol County

Overview

Address
2446 HIGHLAND AVENUE, FALL RIVER, MA 02720
Phone
5086790011
Certified beds
300
Avg daily residents
217 (72% of beds filled)
Ownership
Non-profit (church-affiliated)
Provider type
Medicare and Medicaid
Medicare/Medicaid since
1990-07-01
Setting
Urban
3 /5
★★★★★
CMS Overall Rating

CMS 5-Star Ratings

CMS rates every Medicare/Medicaid-certified nursing home on four domains. The Overall rating is driven primarily by Health Inspection results, then adjusted up or down by Staffing and Quality Measures.

Overall Rating
Composite of Health Inspection, Staffing, and Quality Measures.
★★★★★ 3/5
Health Inspection
Based on the three most recent standard surveys, with more weight on recent results.
★★★★★ 2/5
Staffing
Weighted combination of RN and total nurse staffing hours per resident per day, case-mix adjusted.
★★★★★ 5/5
Quality Measures
15 resident-level quality measures split between long-stay and short-stay (post-acute) residents.
★★★★★ 3/5
Quality Measures breakdown
Long-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 4/5
Short-Stay Quality Measures ★★★★★ 2/5

Staffing & Workforce

Direct-care staffing is the strongest operational driver of quality in nursing homes. Values are hours per resident per day, derived from payroll-based journal (PBJ) submissions. "Case-mix" adjusts for resident acuity; "Adjusted" is the CMS rating-input value.

Role Reported Case-mix expected Adjusted Federal floor
Total nurse
All nursing staff combined: RN + LPN + Aide
 4.14 3.39 4.72 ≥ 3.48
Registered Nurse (RN)
Licensed RN hours. Strongest driver of clinical outcomes.
 0.60 0.59 0.68 ≥ 0.55
Licensed Practical Nurse (LPN)
LPN/LVN hours. Often handles medication administration.
 0.94 0.75 1.07 —
Nurse aide
CNA hours. Bulk of direct resident care — bathing, feeding, mobility.
 2.60 2.04 2.96 —
Licensed (RN + LPN)
Combined licensed nurse coverage.
 1.54 — — —
Physical therapist
Rehabilitation therapist hours — important for post-acute / rehab admissions.
 0.03 — — —

Federal minimums (phasing in under the CMS 2024 minimum staffing rule) shown for reference. RN: 0.55 hrs/resident/day. Total nurse: 3.48 hrs/resident/day.

Weekend staffing

Total nurse (weekend)
3.86
hrs / resident / day
RN (weekend)
0.45
hrs / resident / day
Total nurse (adjusted, weekend)
4.40
hrs / resident / day

Weekend under-staffing is a common quality-of-care concern — adverse events are more frequent when licensed coverage drops.

Staff turnover

Total nursing staff turnover
28.3%
annual
RN turnover
25.0%
annual
Administrators departed
0
last 12 months

Resident acuity

Nursing Case-Mix Index
1.20
higher acuity than avg
Case-Mix Index Ratio
0.87
facility / state ratio

Health Inspections

CMS weights three inspection cycles to compute the Health Inspection rating: the most recent (50%), the second most recent (33%), and the oldest (17%). Each standard-survey deficiency is assigned a score based on scope and severity; complaint-survey findings and revisit scores are added to produce the cycle total.

Cycle Date Total defs. Standard Complaint Deficiency score Revisits Total score
Cycle 1 (most recent) 2025-04-17 10 8 2 80 1 80
Cycle 2/3 (prior) 2024-03-26 8 8 0 152 1 152
Total weighted health score
98.0
lower is better

Deficiencies (42)

Individual survey findings. Scope/severity uses the CMS A–L matrix: letters further down the alphabet indicate greater harm and wider scope, up through J–L (immediate jeopardy).

37 D/E/F Actual harm — potential for minor
5 G/H/I Actual harm
Tag Description Scope/Severity Survey date Corrected
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. G 2025-10-14 2025-11-14
0689 Ensure that a nursing home area is free from accident hazards and provides adequate supervision to prevent accidents. G 2025-10-14 2025-11-14
0550 Honor the resident's right to a dignified existence, self-determination, communication, and to exercise his or her rights. E 2025-04-17 2025-05-19
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-19
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-19
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-19
0810 Provide special eating equipment and utensils for residents who need them and appropriate assistance. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-19
0812 Procure food from sources approved or considered satisfactory and store, prepare, distribute and serve food in accordance with professional standards. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-19
0842 Safeguard resident-identifiable information and/or maintain medical records on each resident that are in accordance with accepted professional standards. D 2025-04-17 2025-05-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. E 2025-04-17 2025-05-19
0600 Protect each resident from all types of abuse such as physical, mental, sexual abuse, physical punishment, and neglect by anybody. H 2024-03-26 2024-04-12
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. H 2024-03-26 2024-04-12
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. E 2024-03-26 2024-04-12
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. H 2024-03-26 2024-04-12
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2024-03-26 2024-04-12
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2024-03-26 2024-04-12
0740 Ensure each resident must receive and the facility must provide necessary behavioral health care and services. E 2024-03-26 2024-04-12
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. E 2024-03-26 2024-04-12
0554 Allow residents to self-administer drugs if determined clinically appropriate. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0583 Keep residents' personal and medical records private and confidential. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0604 Ensure that each resident is free from the use of physical restraints, unless needed for medical treatment. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0607 Develop and implement policies and procedures to prevent abuse, neglect, and theft. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0609 Timely report suspected abuse, neglect, or theft and report the results of the investigation to proper authorities. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0610 Respond appropriately to all alleged violations. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0620 Not require residents to give up Medicare or Medicaid benefits, or pay privately as a condition of admission; and must tell residents what care they do not provide. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0656 Develop and implement a complete care plan that meets all the resident's needs, with timetables and actions that can be measured. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0657 Develop the complete care plan within 7 days of the comprehensive assessment; and prepared, reviewed, and revised by a team of health professionals. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0658 Ensure services provided by the nursing facility meet professional standards of quality. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0685 Assist a resident in gaining access to vision and hearing services. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0686 Provide appropriate pressure ulcer care and prevent new ulcers from developing. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0695 Provide safe and appropriate respiratory care for a resident when needed. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0697 Provide safe, appropriate pain management for a resident who requires such services. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0742 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with mental disorder or psychosocial adjustment difficulty, or who has a history of trauma and/or post-traumatic stress disorder. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0744 Provide the appropriate treatment and services to a resident who displays or is diagnosed with dementia. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0756 Ensure a licensed pharmacist perform a monthly drug regimen review, including the medical chart, following irregularity reporting guidelines in developed policies and procedures. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0757 Ensure each resident’s drug regimen must be free from unnecessary drugs. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0758 Implement gradual dose reductions(GDR) and non-pharmacological interventions, unless contraindicated, prior to initiating or instead of continuing psychotropic medication; and PRN orders for psychotropic medications are only used when the medication is necessary and PRN use is limited. E 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0761 Ensure drugs and biologicals used in the facility are labeled in accordance with currently accepted professional principles; and all drugs and biologicals must be stored in locked compartments, separately locked, compartments for controlled drugs. F 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0849 Arrange for the provision of hospice services or assist the resident in transferring to a facility that will arrange for the provision of hospice services. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0880 Provide and implement an infection prevention and control program. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0881 Implement a program that monitors antibiotic use. D 2022-06-29 2022-08-19
0908 Keep all essential equipment working safely. E 2022-06-29 2022-08-19

Methodology & sources
  • Nursing Home Provider Info (nh-provider-info), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,703 rows.
  • Nursing Home Penalties (nh-penalties), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 16,915 rows.
  • Nursing Home Health Deficiencies (nh-deficiencies), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 418,972 rows.
  • Nursing Home Ownership (nh-ownership), vintage 2026, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 160,393 rows.
  • Skilled Nursing Facility Cost Report (snf-cost-report), vintage 2023, downloaded 2026-04-14 , 14,120 rows.
